title: pellaea woodfordii
intro: Pellaea woodfordii, also known as Wood's fern or Australian tree fern, is a species of fern native to eastern Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pellaea woodfordii include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. Pellaea woodfordii can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, and it is also used in traditional medicine practices to treat various ailments, including colds, fever, and skin conditions.
